title: The Copywriter's Handbook
subtitle: A Step-by-Step Guide to Writing Copy That Sells
author: Robert W. Bly
themes: Headlines are 80% of the ad's value
Know your prospect's beliefs, desires, and feelings
awards: Gold Echo Award, Direct Marketing Association
ETR Lifetime Achievement Award in Marketing
Voted one of 50 most influential people in sales lead management
excerpt: The headline is the most important element in most advertisements. It decides whether the reader will read the copy. On average, five times as many people read the headline as read the body copy. When you have written your headline, you have spent eighty cents out of your dollar.
